_meta.diagnosed_by: eval_harness.hexa not usable (synthetic proxy); used existing kr_gen.py on pod against real checkpoints

eval_harness_status.reason: eval_harness.hexa is a SYNTHETIC deterministic proxy — computes macro_acc via logistic(step/500) on hardcoded KoBEST/HAE-RAE/MMLU-Ko reference scores; does not actually load checkpoints or run lm_eval. Running it would return identical numbers for r4 and r9 regardless of weights. Not a valid collapse diagnosis tool.
eval_harness_status.proposed_stub: Use the existing /workspace/kr_gen.py (already on pod, 5-prompt greedy generation) as minimum eval — pattern-level collapse detection without HF lm_eval dependency.

pod_state.r4_resume_note: /workspace/ckpt_r4_resume/ does NOT exist on pod (cleaned or never copied). R4 step_1000 adapter (201MB only, not 28GB base) fetched from r2:anima-models/alm14b/r4/step_1000/ to /workspace/ckpt_r4_step1000/ for real comparison.

macro_acc_note: Not computed — eval_harness.hexa is synthetic, and running real HF lm_eval would take hours per checkpoint. Used kr_gen as collapse proxy instead.

r9_training_evidence.resume_evidence: Log shows loss=16.0463 at step=1 (fresh-init LoRA) and 'trainable params: 50,331,648 || 0.3396%'. No peft_load_adapter or resume message. r9 trained FROM SCRATCH despite intent to resume from r4 or a2_tool_mixed.

eval_loss_curve.note: Best at step_5000; never improved for remaining 5000 steps. Classic overfit + mode collapse plateau.

r9_training_evidence.ce_loss_note: ckpt_meta ce_loss=2.416 is the FROZEN best-ever (step 5000). Actual step_10000 eval_loss was 2.76 — 15% worse than best.
r9_training_evidence.corpus_note: Same corpus family as A2-Tool training that collapsed per project_a2_tool_mode_collapse_20260417. r4 used same corpus and converged to loss=1.50 at step_1000; r9 started fresh and reached only 2.42 at step_5000 then regressed.

r4_training_evidence.consciousness_loss_b1: unknown (r4 train_meta lacks field — likely disabled)
r4_training_evidence.kr_gen_quality: partial — fragmented Korean with unicode spam, but still decodes to ~50% readable tokens; NOT full mode collapse

diagnosis: r9 is in MODE COLLAPSE (pure token repetition — JSON braces, unicode garbage, single-char loops). r4 step_1000 is partially degraded but still produces recognizable Korean fragments. The critical training signal: r9 loss=16 at step=1 proves it started from SCRATCH, NOT resumed from r4 or a2_tool_mixed. Combined with (a) consciousness_loss_b1 ENABLED (not in r4), (b) 10x longer training (10000 steps vs 1000), (c) LR decayed to 5e-08, (d) eval_loss regression after step=5000, the model overfit on the chatml corpus and memorized the JSON message-format delimiters rather than the conversational content. Cosine-schedule pinned to 10k steps on a 27M-token corpus gave ~8 epochs — far into overfit territory for LoRA r=32 on 14B.
root_cause_hypothesis: PRIMARY: r9 was never actually resumed from r4 — it trained fresh for 10k steps on the same alm70b_chatml corpus that already produced A2-Tool collapse (92% malformed per project_a2_tool_mode_collapse_20260417). The corpus's JSON chatml wrapper (`{"role":"...","messages":[...]}`) was memorized as surface structure, leaking into generation (r9 kr_gen emits raw `{"":""}` JSON fragments and `roleassistant` tokens). SECONDARY: consciousness_loss_b1 (holo=0.01 + cplx=0.005 + gwt=0.005) added ~0.02 of spurious gradient noise per step; phi_holo oscillating 3397–12141 across evals shows the auxiliary loss is not converging, likely fighting the main CE objective. TERTIARY: Training 10000 steps with LR cosine decay to 5e-08 on 27M tokens = severe overfit; best eval at step=5000 then monotonic regression through step=10000 is the textbook overfit signature.

r10_plan.verify_resume: Add [resume] log line after PeftModel.from_pretrained — first-step loss MUST be <3.0 (not 16). Abort if loss>5 at step=1.
r10_plan.disable_b1: Set consciousness_loss_b1=false for r10. Re-enable only after a B1-off baseline converges cleanly. Phi_holo oscillation 3k–12k proves B1 is unstable on 14B+chatml.
r10_plan.steps_cap: Max 2000 steps (2 epochs over 27M tokens at seq=1024). r4 reached loss=1.50 at step_1000; r9's best was 2.42 at step_5000 — both signals say <=2000 is the sweet spot.
r10_plan.corpus_filter: Filter alm70b_chatml corpus to strip {"role":"alm70b_chatml"} JSON wrapper before tokenization — feed only the inner messages text. Current corpus encodes the JSON envelope as training tokens, which the model then emits at inference.
r10_plan.lr_schedule: constant 2e-6 or cosine 3e-6 -> 1e-6 over 2000 steps (r4 used 5e-6 and overshot in r9; 2e-6 keeps LoRA updates in-distribution).
r10_plan.sanity_eval_cadence: kr_gen every 200 steps, abort on JSON-bracket or single-char repetition detector.
